author: codex-root

patch.value: Exact merged-head CodeQL alert #10 reports js/clear-text-logging at src/commands/config.ts:29. Although current payload builders mask apiKey and userPassword, a generic console.log sink accepts unknown payloads and permits future accidental secret disclosure. Replace it with the CLI structured stdout channel, retain formatting contracts, add regression coverage, and verify the alert closes on the new default-branch head.
Prevent configuration secrets from reaching logging sinks
Eliminate the generic console logging sink used by config commands so masked configuration output remains safe by construction and GitHub code scanning has zero open alerts.

Config output uses a non-logging structured stdout sink; API keys and passwords remain masked in every config read/show/setup/set response; focused and full tests retain exact 100/100/100/100 coverage; CodeQL security workflow succeeds with zero open alerts on the new merged head; package is not published
All-status PM search completed with no duplicate; exact alert, commit, path, and sink identified.
Maintain zero high-severity code-scanning findings on the default branch
Prevents credential disclosure regressions in the local settings interface.
Protects Monica API tokens and user credentials while preserving programmatic output.
The otherwise-green merged API parity release surfaced an exact-head high-severity CodeQL alert.
GitHub Security workflow on merged master 809fe40794bb9f1ce8f18181fcb78fc39115d438
Open GitHub code-scanning alert 10 or run the Security workflow against merged commit 809fe407; inspect src/commands/config.ts line 29.
Configuration commands emit validated structured output without any secret-capable console logging sink; all tests and security scans pass; open CodeQL alerts return to zero.
Merged commit 809fe407 has one open high-severity js/clear-text-logging alert at the generic config printer.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate-safe all-status search found no existing item for CodeQL alert 10. This is a focused post-merge security remediation linked to the API parity delivery and platform epic.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Do not place live credentials in PM metadata, source, tests, commits, PR text, or logs. Verification must use synthetic tokens and GitHub alert metadata only.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A passing CodeQL workflow means analysis completed successfully; it does not imply the repository has zero open findings. Exact-head alert inventory is a separate mandatory release proof.

message: Track exact-head CodeQL config output sink remediation

author: codex-root
message: Keep remediation in the unpublished 2026.7.21-2 release candidate

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation replaces the generic console.log config sink with structured stdout output and adds a regression assertion that masked config reads never invoke console.log. The release remains 2026.7.21-2 because that candidate is merged but intentionally untagged and unpublished.

patch.value: Replaced the generic console.log configuration sink with process.stdout.write of the already-formatted structured payload and added focused regression coverage proving masked config reads do not invoke console.log.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Sensitive-output safety must be enforced both at payload construction and at the final output sink. Tests now assert the config command never routes masked credentials through console logging.

patch.value: Implemented a structured stdout sink, retained masked API key/password output, passed 838 tests with exact 100/100/100/100 coverage, passed 273 help checks and all release/security/history gates, and recorded the exact-head CodeQL follow-up for default-branch verification.
PR #60 exact head 54cd3304e1cf27d14eeda5c586bd86beb402ec82 completed both CodeQL checks successfully and the PR-scoped open code-scanning alert inventory returned zero.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: CodeRabbit correctly requested explicit gated proof. GitHub CodeQL completed successfully twice on PR #60 head 54cd3304e1cf27d14eeda5c586bd86beb402ec82, and GET /code-scanning/alerts?state=open&pr=60 returned an empty alert set. The close event is therefore backed by exact-head security evidence before merge; default-branch inventory will be repeated after merge.
